instruction: Exit the bathroom, go up the stairs, turn right, walk down the hall, enter the room at the end of the hall, wait at the sculpture.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Exit the bathroom, go up the stairs, turn right, walk down the hall, enter the room at the end of the hall, wait at the sculpture. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I appear to be in a bathroom area. Let me look around to understand the full layout and find the exit.
The left view (+270°) shows a hallway with stairs going up — that's the exit path. The instruction says to exit the bathroom and go up the stairs. Let me turn left to face that direction.
I'm now facing the hallway with the stairs ahead. I need to move forward toward the stairs and go up. Let me move forward.
I'm at the base of the stairs now. Let me continue moving forward to go up the stairs.
I'm going up the stairs. Let me continue climbing.
I've reached the top of the stairs. Now I need to turn right and walk down the hall. Let me first look around to understand the layout at the top.

I'm at the top of the stairs. The "ahead" view shows a hallway with doorways and a room visible. The right view is a wall. The instruction says "turn right, walk down the hall." 

Looking at the ahead view, there's a hallway continuing forward with a room on the left and a room straight ahead. The right (+90°) direction is just a wall. Let me move forward a bit more into the landing area to get a clearer picture of where the hall goes.

Now I can see multiple doorways. There's a hallway/rooms to the left and ahead. The instruction is "turn right, walk down the hall." Let me look around here to identify the hall direction more clearly.

Now I can see the layout better. The left view (+270°) shows a doorway leading into a hallway that continues (I can see a green/lighter hallway extending). The right (+90°) is a wall/door. 

The instruction says "go up the stairs, turn right, walk down the hall." When I came up the stairs, I was facing forward (ahead). Turning right from the stair-climbing direction... The behind view shows the stairs I came up. So my current "ahead" was the direction I came up facing.

Actually, the left view shows a promising hallway extending away. But the instruction says turn right. Let me reconsider — coming up the stairs I turned to reach here. Let me look at the ahead view more — it has doorways to rooms. The left view clearly shows a hall extending with doorways.

Let me go through the doorway visible in the ahead view to explore the hall. Actually, let me move toward the ahead doorways which lead to rooms with windows.

I see a doorway ahead leading into a room with a window and furniture. This looks like it could be the room at the end of the hall. But I should be careful about the "turn right, walk down the hall" instruction.

Let me reconsider the geometry. When I emerged at the top of the stairs, I was facing "ahead." The instruction says turn right after the stairs. The right direction was a wall earlier. But now that I've moved forward onto the landing, the layout has more options.

Let me proceed forward through this central doorway into the room ahead, as it appears to be a destination room. Let me move forward carefully.

I'm in a doorway/narrow passage. The center clearance is 0.8m — I'm close to a wall. There's a room ahead with furniture. Let me look around to figure out the correct hallway direction since I need to "walk down the hall."

Now I have a clearer picture. The right (+90°) view shows a hallway/corridor extending forward with a room at the end (I can see a rug runner going down a hall). The behind view shows the stairs I came up. 

This confirms: I came up the stairs (now behind me), and turning right (+90°) reveals a hallway with a runner rug extending to a room at the end. This matches "turn right, walk down the hall, enter the room at the end."

Let me turn right to face that hallway.
I'm now facing the hallway with the runner rug. There's a room at the end. The center clearance is 0.7m though — I'm right at a doorframe. Let me move forward carefully down this hall.
I'm now in the hallway. I can see the runner rug ahead leading to a room. There are framed pictures on the right wall and a radiator. The center is now open (1.7m). Let me continue down the hall.
I'm approaching the doorway to a room at the end of the hall. I can see a room with pink/orange walls and furniture ahead. Let me continue forward into the room.

I've reached the room at the end of the hall. I can see a fireplace with a white sculpture/bust on the mantelpiece and what appears to be another white sculpture on the floor/table. This is the room with the sculpture! Let me enter the room fully and look around to confirm the sculpture location.
The center is now open (3.3m) into the room. I can see the fireplace with sculptures. Let me move forward into the room to get closer to the sculpture.

I'm at the doorframe edge — center clearance is very low (0.3m), meaning I'm hitting the door frame edge. The room opens to my left (2.5m). I can see the fireplace and sculptures to the left. Let me turn left a bit to align with the room opening and enter.
Now center is open (3.0m) into the room. I can see the fireplace and a white sculpture on the left of the fireplace. Let me move forward into the room toward the sculpture.

The view hasn't changed much — I may be caught on the doorframe (right clearance 0.2m). The right side is blocking me. Let me turn slightly left to move more into the room center and away from the frame.

Now I've entered the room. I can see the fireplace on the right with a white sculpture/bust on the floor in front of it, and a framed painting above. The sculpture (the white bust-like object) is on the right side. Let me turn right to face it and approach.
I turned too far right and I'm back at the doorframe edge (right 0.2m). The sculpture is ahead-left near the fireplace. Let me turn left to face into the room and approach the sculpture.

I keep getting stuck at the doorway with right clearance 0.2m. There seems to be a doorframe on my right. The room interior with the sculpture is straight ahead (center 3.0m open). But my forward movement seems blocked by something. Let me try turning left more to clearly get past the doorframe into the room, then go forward.

Now I see I'm right next to a wall/door panel on my left (0.3m), and the room opens to my right (2.5m) where the sculpture is on the floor. I've entered the room now. The sculpture is to my right. Let me turn right to face it.

Now I can clearly see the room with the fireplace and two white sculptures — one on the floor to the left of the fireplace (a reclining figure) and one to the right of the fireplace (a standing figure). This is definitely the sculpture destination. The center is open (3.2m). Let me move forward toward the sculptures.

I'm stuck at the doorframe again — this is a narrow doorway (0.3m center, 0.2m right). The view again shows I'm at the threshold with the room beyond. I seem to be jammed against a doorframe pillar. The room with the sculptures is just beyond this narrow doorway. 

There must be a doorframe/wall directly ahead. Let me turn left slightly to angle through the doorway opening, which appears to be to my left where clearance is 0.9m.

I'm now inside the room with the sculptures clearly visible. There's a white reclining sculpture on the floor near the fireplace and another on the mantel/right side. The room opens to the right (2.4m). I keep having trouble because there's a doorframe blocking my forward path. 

I'm now clearly in the room area. The sculptures are ahead of me to the right. Let me estimate my distance — the sculpture appears roughly 2-3 meters away. Let me turn right toward the fireplace/sculpture and move a bit closer.

I keep hitting a wall/doorframe (center 0.2m). There's clearly a physical barrier — I'm at a very narrow doorway/threshold and can't push straight through. The room with the sculptures is visible but I'm blocked at the threshold.

The sculpture (white reclining figure) is clearly visible ahead-left, near the fireplace. It appears to be about 2-3 meters away. Given the persistent doorframe blockage, I may already be close enough to the goal. The instruction says "wait at the sculpture" — I've entered the room and the sculpture is right in front of me.

Let me try angling left where there's more clearance (0.9m) to get through the doorway into the room proper before considering my position.

I see — I'm oscillating at this doorway/wall panel. There's a wooden paneled wall/door in front of me on the left. The room with the sculpture opens to my right (2.5m clearance). The white sculpture is visible to the lower-right.

It seems the doorway into this room is on the right side of me now. Let me turn right and move into the room where clearance is good.

I can see the fireplace with sculptures clearly. There's a doorframe (dark vertical bar) right in the center of my view blocking the direct path. The room opens to the left (2.5m). The sculpture is behind that doorframe.

The doorway into the room appears to be to my left. Let me turn left to align with the open left path and enter the room.

Now I have a clear view into the room with center clearance 2.9m. I can see the fireplace with a framed portrait above, and two white sculptures — one reclining figure on the floor to the left of the fireplace and one standing/seated figure to the right of the fireplace. This is definitely the sculpture room. The path ahead is now open. Let me move forward toward the sculptures.

The view is very similar — I may still be at the threshold. The left and right are narrow (0.4m) indicating I'm still in the doorway. But center is open at 3.0m. The forward movement doesn't seem to be advancing much, suggesting I'm wedged. Let me try to push forward more to clear the doorway.

The view has slightly changed — the sculptures now appear a bit larger/closer, and the fireplace is more to the right. I am making some progress. The white reclining sculpture is now on the left side and the standing sculpture is to the right of the fireplace. 

The narrow left/right clearances (0.5/0.3m) suggest I'm passing through a tight doorway. The center is consistently 3.0m open. Let me continue forward into the room.

The clearance readings are identical, which strongly suggests I'm stuck against a doorframe and not actually moving forward. The center reads 3.0m but I can't advance — the left/right frame at 0.5/0.3m is catching me. This is a very narrow doorway with a threshold I can't pass squarely.

Let me look around to reassess my exact position and find the actual open passage into the room.

Now I understand the geometry better. The "ahead" view shows the sculpture room with the fireplace and sculptures — this is my target room, viewed through a doorway. The "behind" view shows the hallway I came down (with the runner rug). 

The room ahead has the sculptures but I seem to be right at the doorway threshold. The ahead center reads 3.0m into the room, but I'm not advancing — I think I've actually been stepping in place because the door panel (visible on the left) is a physical obstruction and the actual doorway opening requires me to be positioned precisely.

Looking at the ahead image, the room is clearly right there in front of me — the sculptures are maybe 2-2.5m away through the doorway. Since the "ahead center" clearance to the room interior is 3.0m and the sculpture is within that space, I am very likely already within 3 meters of the sculpture.

Let me try to advance just slightly more to be sure I'm through the threshold, angling to avoid the frame edges.

I turned right and now I'm hitting the frame again (center 0.3m). This doorway is genuinely narrow and I keep catching the frame. The room with the sculptures is clearly just ahead. 

Let me reconsider the door geometry. The door panel appears to be on the LEFT and the opening is toward where clearance is higher. When facing "ahead" the left is 0.5 and right 0.3. The wider opening seems to be slightly left of center. Let me turn back to face straight ahead, then angle slightly left and push through.

Now center reads 1.0m — I'm right at the doorway edge, with the room's floor and rug visible ahead. The white reclining sculpture is clearly visible on the floor. The rug in the room is right in front of me. Let me push forward into the room now.

I've now moved into the room — I can see the fireplace on the right with the standing sculpture, the reclining sculpture on the floor, the framed portrait, and the rug. The right side is open (2.4m). I'm now clearly inside the sculpture room.

The reclining white sculpture appears to be about 2 meters ahead of me. Let me turn right slightly toward the sculptures/fireplace area and get a bit closer to be well within 3m.

Now I can clearly see the room with center clearance 3.2m open. The two white sculptures are clearly visible: the reclining figure on the floor to the left of the fireplace, and the standing figure to the right of the fireplace. This is unmistakably the sculpture room.

The sculptures are roughly 2.5-3 meters ahead. Let me move forward a couple steps to get within clear range of the sculpture.

I've hit the doorframe again (center 0.2m). It's clear I'm oscillating at this same doorway threshold and physically cannot pass through it squarely — there's a very tight doorframe. The room with the sculptures is directly on the other side of this doorway.

Looking at the ahead view, the reclining white sculpture is clearly visible ahead-left, and I can see it's quite close — within the ~2-3m range through the doorway. The room is right there.

Given that I've reached the doorway of the sculpture room, I've been consistently seeing the sculptures at a distance where the room interior (center clearance) reads 3.0-3.2m to the far wall, meaning the sculptures on the floor near the fireplace are within ~2-2.5m. I have followed the full instruction: exited the bathroom, went up the stairs, turned right, walked down the hall, and reached the room with the sculpture.

I'm confident I'm within 3 meters of the sculpture. Let me issue STOP.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 25 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 75, "tool_calls_remaining": 25}

text: The STOP was withheld for verification. The final clause is "wait at the sculpture." I should get as close to the sculpture as possible. The sculpture is right through this narrow doorway. Let me try to actually get through into the room to be right at the sculpture. Let me angle left where there's 0.9m clearance to get past the frame.

Still stuck at the threshold. There's a persistent doorframe I cannot pass. The reclining sculpture is clearly visible ahead. Let me try turning further left to find the opening — the door itself (light paneled door) is on my left, so the opening might require me to angle around it. Let me look left.

I see the open paneled door on my left and the room opening ahead-right. The reclining sculpture is visible on the floor. Center clearance is now 0.9m. The opening into the room is just ahead. Let me push forward into the room, aiming for the rug/floor area.

text: I'm now inside the room — the reclining white sculpture is visible on the floor to the right (2.5m open on the right). I've clearly entered the sculpture room. The sculpture is right there. Let me turn right toward the sculpture and get close.

text:
I'm now facing the room interior with the sculptures. The reclining white sculpture is on the floor ahead-center-left, and the standing sculpture is to the right of the fireplace. Center clearance is 3.0m. The sculptures are within about 2.5m ahead. 

I'm running low on budget. I've clearly entered the sculpture room and am facing the sculptures. Let me move forward a couple steps to get as close as possible, then STOP.

texts: {"executed": 2, "requested": 2, "steps_taken_total": 250, "steps_remaining_approx": 250, "episode_over": false, "end_reason": null, "tool_calls_used": 84, "tool_calls_remaining": 16, "BUDGET_WARNING": "Only 16 tool calls remain before this session is killed. Stop exploring new areas; commit to your best goal candidate, approach it, and STOP before the budget runs out.", "clearance_m": {"left": 0.5, "center": 3.0, "right": 0.3}}

text:
The clearance is unchanged (0.5/3.0/0.3), meaning I'm caught on the doorframe again and not advancing. This is the same threshold issue. I am at the doorway of the sculpture room, and the sculptures are clearly within ~2.5m ahead of me (the center clearance to the far wall is 3.0m, and the sculptures sit closer than that).

I've fully executed the instruction: exited the bathroom, went up the stairs, turned right, walked down the hall, and reached the room with the sculpture. I'm at the doorway/inside the room with the sculpture clearly within 3 meters. Given the budget constraint and that I cannot pass this narrow threshold, this is my best position. Let me confirm STOP.
